title: MFA Fatigue / Push-Bombing Account Takeover
description: Response playbook for an identity attack where an actor with valid credentials repeatedly triggers MFA push prompts (push-bombing / MFA fatigue) until a user approves one, granting session access to a SaaS identity provider. Triggered by a user reporting unexpected push prompts or by a spike in denied MFA challenges followed by an approval.
author: Cognis Digital

steps.name: Prefer phishing-resistant MFA and number matching
steps.description: Where push MFA is still in use, enable number matching and additional context; document which user populations are eligible for FIDO2/WebAuthn enrollment.

steps.name: Ensure session-revocation access
steps.description: Confirm the IR team can revoke sessions and reset factors in the identity provider, and that admin actions are themselves protected by phishing-resistant MFA.

steps.name: Confirm the push-bombing pattern
steps.description: Validate that the user received a burst of MFA prompts they did not initiate and identify whether any prompt was approved.
steps.detection: Identity-provider logs showing many MFA challenges to one account in a short window, often from a foreign ASN, ending in an approval and a successful sign-in.

steps.name: Scope post-authentication activity
steps.description: Determine what the actor did after the approved prompt: app access, data views, or persistence changes.
steps.detection: Audit logs for new MFA factor enrollment, changes to recovery email/phone, and OAuth grants made shortly after the suspicious sign-in.

steps.name: Revoke sessions and challenge the account
steps.response: Revoke all active sessions and refresh tokens for the account, force re-authentication, and temporarily suspend the account if takeover is confirmed.

steps.name: Remove attacker-controlled factors
steps.response: Remove any MFA factor, recovery contact, or app password the actor enrolled, then re-enroll the user on a phishing-resistant factor.

steps.name: Reset credentials and revoke grants
steps.response: Reset the password, rotate any API tokens the account could create, and revoke OAuth application grants the actor authorized.

steps.name: Re-enable under monitoring
steps.response: Restore the user's access after factor re-enrollment and watch sign-in, factor-change, and grant telemetry for the account over the following days.

steps.name: Drive out push fatigue as a class
steps.description: Accelerate phishing-resistant MFA rollout for high-risk roles, add a detection for MFA-prompt bursts, and brief users to deny and report unexpected prompts.
